concerns.text: FR-001 requires site selection based on '>80% cloud-free coverage in spring 2020'. Task T011 implements downloading data but lacks a specific task to implement the 2020 cloud-coverage filtering logic to select the 10-15 sites. Without this, the dataset selection criteria in FR-001 is unimplemented.

FR-003 requires mapping ground-truth observations to specific study sites. Task T013 mentions fetching data but does not explicitly include the logic to map/associate these observations to the selected sites defined in FR-001. This mapping is critical for US-1 acceptance scenario 3.

SC-001 requires measuring prediction accuracy against a 'simple linear regression model using only temperature data' as a baseline. No task exists to implement this baseline model or the comparison logic. T024 only calculates metrics on the primary model.

SC-002 requires comparing generalization error against training error to quantify overfitting. While T025 mentions reporting generalization error, there is no explicit task to calculate the training set error and perform the comparison required by SC-002.

SC-004 requires measuring predictor importance by the 'increase in RMSE... when each feature is permuted versus excluded'. Task T029 implements permutation importance but does not explicitly include the 'excluded' (zeroed) comparison logic required to measure the specific metric defined in SC-004.

Edge Case (missing phenology labels) requires masking rows during training. Task T016 mentions this, but the Edge Case also specifies handling 'zero cloud-free satellite observations for a critical window' by flagging the site as 'insufficient data' and excluding it. T015 handles cloud coverage but does not explicitly cover the 'critical window' logic defined in the Edge Case section.

Task T007 places `provenance.yaml` in `src/data/`. The Plan and Constitution Principle VI explicitly require the provenance file to be under `data/` (e.g., `data/provenance.yaml`) to link with raw data artifacts. This is a stale path from an older structure.
